title: WSET Knowledge Map — Misconception
description: A documented incorrect belief that learners systematically hold about a WSET L3 concept. Misconceptions are distinct from random errors — they are predictable, recurring cognitive patterns.

misconception_id.description: Stable unique identifier. Format: MC_{CONCEPT_SLUG}_{N:02d} — e.g. MC_TANNIN_01

misconception.description: The incorrect belief, stated precisely as a learner would hold it. Use first person or declarative form.

why_incorrect.description: Technical explanation of why this belief is wrong. Must be specific, not just 'this is false'.

corrected_understanding.description: The accurate replacement belief. Should be expressible as a single clear statement.

related_topics.description: List of topic_ids where this misconception is likely to arise.

related_concepts.description: List of concept_ids most directly affected by this misconception.

severity.description: How damaging this misconception is for exam performance if uncorrected.

frequency.description: Estimated prevalence of this misconception among WSET L3 learners.

distinction_relevance.description: Whether holding this misconception would prevent a learner from reaching Distinction level.

tutor_intervention.description: Recommended Tutor Agent intervention strategy when this misconception is detected.

detection_signals.description: Phrases or answer patterns that signal a learner may hold this misconception.
